key_points: Dorms are tiny, especially classic triples shared by three students
Some students get lucky with single ACs but most suffer cramped conditions
4th-year transfers still stuck on the Hill rooming with freshmen
key_quotes: Bro I can stick my arms out and touch both of the bunk beds in my classic shared triple
If I had a dorm like this freshman year I probably wouldn't have experienced the extreme depressive episode that I did
The dorms are SO SMALL... most other people got placed in a 3 people dorm, but they are the same size

key_points: Campus is politically charged with frequent protests and counter-protests
Bruinwalk described as intimidating for looks and social pressure
Finding friend groups sophomore year is harder than expected
key_quotes: ucla makes me feel ugly... I was above average in high school and get complimented often but here I'm holy chopped
Last year was my freshman year... The past 2 quarters have been the best months of my life
I didn't really vibe with my group of friends and there was a bit of a falling out, so I'm starting fresh this year

key_points: BPlate has confrontational seat-stealing incidents going viral on Reddit
Food quality praised by current students but entitlement among diners is a complaint
No specific dining hall horror stories but student behavior around food draws criticism
key_quotes: To the bitch who 1 took our seats at bplate 2 sat on my BRIGHT GREEN IPAD 3 had the audacity to act surprised
but it's great here, the food is amazing!!
these are the people who's parents taught them no basic manners whatsoever

key_points: Grade deflation causes genuine psychological harm, not just stress
Students describe depression, isolation, and loneliness as common first-year experiences
Quarter system pace leaves little time to recover between breakdowns
key_quotes: we had grade deflation in my uni too and it's fucking insane. i got a 92% once and it was a C+. they're literally asking for students to have mental breakdowns
College is where I saw so many people with depression, anxiety and just terrible mental health
when u said u cried because u felt lonelier than ever, i FELT that. istg we're really on our own out here

key_points: OOS students pay ~$45k/year with little to no merit aid
Financial aid paperwork delays can leave incoming freshmen without award letters
Community college transfer route seen as smarter financial play by many
key_quotes: I was accepted to ucla straight from high school and dropped out after 2 months bc it was way too expensive
UCLA costs 45k vs Columbia being 30k
I paid 1500 for a semester of community college and 5000 for a semester of a big name in-state university

key_points: Grade deflation is real and systemic, not just rumor
Class registration is cutthroat, classes fill instantly and some students can't graduate on time
Rampant cheating in large lecture courses with TAs and professors ignoring it
key_quotes: Rampant cheating in math department... the back half of the class has been on their phones copying chatgpt answers... TAs and prof don't seem to notice or care
My soul was getting crushed just watching this... Why accept more students if there isn't enough staff for it. It's a total scam
I went there and they dont care if you graduate or not. you are an ant in an ocean. Open enrollment still gives me trauma over 20 years later

key_points: UCLA admin's silence on faculty misconduct scandals signals institutional indifference
Federal funding battle with Trump administration creates real uncertainty for research students
Admissions scandals including fake athletic recruits and Epstein-connected faculty damage trust
key_quotes: this kind of silence signals to vulnerable students that UCLA is NOT a safe place
Estimated $200 million in research funding to UCLA cancelled by the federal government
They're gonna tank the value of my degree right as I go on the job market

key_points: Campus is widely considered beautiful, a genuine selling point
Westwood location gives access to LA but also brings LA problems nearby
Free bus to LAX via U-Pass is a hidden practical perk students love
key_quotes: Just arrived at UCLA. Why is the school so freaking beautiful
UCLA is literally surrounded by ultra wealthy people
Your U-Pass gives you fare-free access to the Culver City 6 which takes you directly to the LAX/Metro Transit Center. $0, no stress

key_points: Film school is elite but Hollywood connections still depend on nepotism
Pre-med culture is overwhelming and toxic, with students dogging on other majors
Alumni network includes 12 billionaires but day-to-day career support is inconsistent
key_quotes: executives in their offices doing cocaine and people getting jobs because their dad or their cousin worked somewhere
why is everyone pre med here and lowkey why are so many mad annoying and arrogant. i would NOT want you to be my doctor
There are 12 Bruin billionaires putting UCLA at #17 worldwide

key_points: OOS students question whether UCLA prestige justifies $45k+ price over merit scholarship offers elsewhere
Grade deflation undercuts GPA-dependent goals like med school for the tuition paid
In-state cost is competitive but class access problems mean paying for classes you can't get into
key_quotes: Would I be dumb for turning down Harvard? The main reason I am debating not accepting is the distance
Pass on a top school? My daughter loved it but I'm not convinced the prestige of the school equals good value. Will have to pay OOS tuition
And 5 to 10 years down the line, none of it will matter cause you will be working alongside and maybe FOR people who went to community college

key_points: Beautiful campus, LA location, and strong brand create genuine excitement
Underneath the hype, students feel like numbers in a system that doesn't care if they graduate
Politically intense environment that not everyone is prepared for
key_quotes: The past 2 quarters have been the best months of my life. It is an indescribable experience to be surrounded by such kind, passionate, and intelligent people 24/7
I went there and they dont care if you graduate or not. you are an ant in an ocean
I feel like I just watched a mini movie. How did she manage to make me laugh, cry, feel inspired

key_points: Faculty misconduct: Professor forwarded female undergrad names to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein; admin stayed silent for weeks
Cheating epidemic: Open ChatGPT cheating during exams with TAs and professors doing nothing
Federal funding crisis: $200M in research grants cancelled, creating major uncertainty for STEM students
key_quotes: He violated FERPA and his students' trust. He repeatedly BCCed Epstein on emails about his classes. He sent information about undergraduates to a convicted sex offender
the back half of the class has been on their phones copying chatgpt answers on the past two exams... TAs and prof don't seem to notice or care
Estimated $200 million in research funding to UCLA cancelled by the federal government

key_points: Free LAX transit via U-Pass saves students real money on every trip home
Powell Cat now has an official statue at Kerckhoff Hall, beloved campus tradition
Gaming lounge has RTX 4070 PCs at 360hz monitors, genuinely high-end setup
key_quotes: Your U-Pass gives you fare-free access to the Culver City 6 which takes you directly to the LAX/Metro Transit Center. $0, no stress
The Powell Cat Statue is now at the main entrance of UCLA Kerckhoff Hall
The pc's are high performance with each having an Nvidia RTX 4070 gpu, and the monitor specs are 1080p, 360hz
